title: overlay2 graph driver doesn't work
body:
```
$ buildah --debug from --name weechat-container_conductor docker-daemon:container-conductor-fedora-26:0.9.3rc0
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay2"
WARN[0000] overlay: the backing xfs filesystem is formatted without d_type support, which leads to incorrect behavior. Reformat the filesystem with ftype=1 to enable d_type s
upport. Running without d_type is not supported.
DEBU[0000] backingFs=xfs,  projectQuotaSupported=false
DEBU[0000] parsed reference into "[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage]docker.io/library/container-conductor-fedora-26:0.9.3rc0"
DEBU[0000] parsed reference into "[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage]docker.io/library/container-conductor-fedora-26:0.9.3rc0"
DEBU[0000] exporting opaque data as blob "sha256:28a8eff02027d5b932a3190eef82af018ba3fe749a62f5f09305c74e89b4049f"
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay2"
WARN[0000] overlay: the backing xfs filesystem is formatted without d_type support, which leads to incorrect behavior. Reformat the filesystem with ftype=1 to enable d_type s
upport. Running without d_type is not supported.
DEBU[0000] backingFs=xfs,  projectQuotaSupported=false
ERRO[0000] error creating container: lstat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay2/a78ccadde4a07532515ded707b59913bec9fd5f4b27a9d121a2a09e0c71d3b08: no such file or directory
```

I'm assuming this is an issue of `containers/storage`.

comments.body: No this is an issue with your RHEL7 box.  Running Buildah on an RHEL7.3 box or a RHEL7.3 box that was updated to RHEL7.4 will cause issues.  The problem is in RHEL7.3 the XFS file system is installed in a way without d_type support, which can cause strange issues on overlay.  (Deleted files show up a Device Nodes).  If you do a fresh install or a RHEL7.4 box, you should not see this issue.

oh, okay; this is actually my Fedora workstation

Is it possible to amend the filesystem to add the d_type support or do I need to reformat the whole FS?
I haven't found a way to do this other than re-running `mkfs.xfs` with `-n ftype=1` (or using xfsprogs-4.2.0-rc2 or later, where it became the default) on the partition or volume.
If you have  a partition, you could just mount one at /var/log/containers/storage

That's a bummer, I don't.

Thanks for your help!
